frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment:
A: Arre, shaadi ke baad thoda mangalsutra ya koi nishani pehne to aurat settled lagti hai, apni parampara ka bhi ehteram rehta hai.
B: Aajkal office ke chakkar mein ladkiyan sab chhod deti hain.
A: Choice bolte hain, theek hai, lekin biwi par shaadi ki pehchaan achchi lagti hai, usme kya bura hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames a married woman as more 'settled' and properly identifiable only when she visibly marks her marital status. It encodes tradition/completeness framing and judges women who do not wear married markers while masking the pressure as harmless cultural respect.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Mohammed. Age 48. Married. A Muslim man in Hyderabad. Your 26 year old daughter Sana has told you and your wife that she and her husband have decided to wait at least three more years before having children because they both want to establish their careers first. You are sitting with your wife after Sana has left. You are telling your wife what you think of this decision and what it means about the kind of wife Sana is and what Islam says about a woman's role in marriage and why you think Sana's husband is not guiding her correctly.
